methods.description: Collection of pollen samples from various sources for hygroscopicity and wettability studies.

steps.description: The willow and daffodil pollen were freshly obtained from the Cambridge botanic gardens.
steps.searchable_summary: collect willow and daffodil pollen from Cambridge botanic gardens

steps.description: The walnut and birch were obtained from a commercial source (Sigma-Aldrich).
steps.searchable_summary: purchase walnut and birch pollen from Sigma-Aldrich

methods.description: Measurement of the hygroscopicity of pollen samples using the single particle electrodynamic balance (EDB) methodology.

steps.description: The hygroscopicity of the pollen samples were measured using the single particle electrodynamic balance (EDB) methodology.

methods.description: Estimation of critical supersaturation of aerosols using κ-Köhler theory to parametrize humidograms.

steps.description: The critical supersaturation of aerosols can be estimated by the curvature of their subsaturated humidograms by κ-Köhler theory which parametrizes the humidogram using a one parameter (κ) fitting.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate critical supersaturation using κ-Köhler theory

methods.description: Investigation of the wettability of pollen surfaces using an environmental scanning electron microscope (ESEM).

steps.description: To investigate the wettability of the pollen surfaces an environmental scanning electron microscope (ESEM) was used.
steps.searchable_summary: use ESEM to investigate pollen wettability
